title: Summarize a passage in 3 bullets
response:
- Before the 1950s, cargo was shipped via break-bulk — sacks, barrels, and crates loaded piece by piece — so loading a medium-sized ship could take a week and cost about $5.86 per ton.
- In 1956, trucking entrepreneur Malcom McLean sent the converted tanker Ideal X from Newark to Houston carrying 58 uniform metal boxes liftable straight from truck to ship, cutting loading costs to roughly 16 cents per ton.
- The real breakthrough was standardization — international standards agreed by 1968 settled common dimensions and twistlock corner castings so any crane in any port could handle any container — driving shipping costs so low that transport largely stopped mattering to where goods were made, reshaping global trade and making far-flung supply chains economical.
scoreDetail: fidelity: 9/10, coverage: 9/10, format compliance: 9/10, concision: 6/10 — Accurate and complete but the third bullet is a long run-on stuffed with clauses.
